name: Tallulah Bankhead
abstract: Tallulah Brockman Bankhead (January 31, 1902 – December 12, 1968) was an American actress. Primarily an actress of the stage, Bankhead also appeared in several films including an award-winning performance in Alfred Hitchcock's Lifeboat (1944).

concerts.label: Anniversary of Pearl Harbor Program: "The Infamy of the Axis"

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Bushkin... brought a beautiful long-stemmed rose, which he said he had just come from visiting with Tallulah Bankhead and this a gift from Tallulah. Louis, he smelled the rose... Inside the rose, he pulled out this beautifully rolled long joint that was a gift from Tallulah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: They would have about 20 minutes of Tallulah Bankhead or somebody like that from the show that she had... and it would have to have background music.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: So people like the Duke and Duchess of Windsor would come in, Tallulah Bankhead, Ronald Reagan and his wife... they all want to see me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: She was one of my staunch supporters. She liked blues and she liked jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: People like Tallulah Bankhead were in the club, swooning at his feet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
